hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) interactions through electrostatic repulsion, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid, but the focus should shift to improving structural quality and enforcing selectivity through targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with weak binding to pass. Tightening the ddG cutoff will ensure only high-affinity binders are considered.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations at these positions will help break off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore.
